calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-02-2025 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA, INC.'s Motion for Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Off calendar. Dismissal of entire action of all parties and all causes of action with prejudice filed December 3, 2024.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint, Declaration Of Arkady Itkin
Off Calendar Nov-04-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JOSHUA JORDAN's Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is denied. The motion is based on plaintiff's assertion that, at his August 20, 2024 deposition, it became "apparent for the first time to Plaintiff and his counsel that Plaintiff was terminated just six days after submitting his formal request for religious accommodation on March 29, 2023." (Memo. 3:10-14.) Actually, plaintiff pled that precise information in his April 17, 2023 complaint and so was aware of it at least 18 months ago. (Cmplt. 3:20-4:4.) Liberal amendment of pleadings is common in California, but a court may exercise its discretion to deny where, as here, the moving party has been dilatory. (Crocker Nat. Bank v. Emerald (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 852, 864.) As to prejudice, defendant has a summary adjudication motion pending. Taking discovery on a new retaliation cause of action in order to file a new motion would add expense and push trial from January 27 deep into 2025. (See Magpli v. Farmers Group, Inc. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 471, 486-88.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
